summary: Top 6 +EV picks · MLB & NBA Player Props

picks.reasoning: The model projects Ohtani for a median of 2.0 total bases, giving the Over 1.5 a 57% implied win probability. BetMGM is pricing this at -110 while the consensus market sits at -120 to -130 — that gap represents genuine line value. Ohtani has cleared 1.5 total bases in 18 of his last 25 starts against right-handed pitching, and Miami starter Sandy Alcantara is coming off two shaky outings. The combination of a favorable projection, a soft line relative to consensus, and a good recent trend makes this the top play today.

The model's Poisson projection gives Glasnow a median of 6.69 strikeouts, with roughly 64% of the distribution landing under 7.5. Despite the heavy juice at -147, the edge holds — implied odds from that price are only 59.5%, leaving a 4.5-point gap. Glasnow has gone under this number in 4 of his last 6 starts and averages 5.9 IP per outing this season, capping his strikeout ceiling. Miami's lineup is swing-happy but makes enough contact to keep the K rate in check.

Duren's model median sits at 9.8 rebounds — 0.71 units below the 10.5 line. Using a normal distribution (σ ≈ 2.2), that projects to about 61% Under probability. The -132 price implies only 56.9%, giving us a clean edge. Orlando plays at one of the slower paces in the league, limiting total possession count and, by extension, rebound opportunities. Duren has also gone under 10.5 boards in 5 of his last 7 games. Available at Sportsbook Rhode Island.

Sengun's median projection of 9.06 rebounds puts roughly 58.7% of outcomes under 9.5. The -132 line implies 56.9%, so the edge is narrow but positive. LA's frontcourt tends to box out aggressively, and Sengun has been limited in rebound opportunities against physical big men this season. This is a complementary play to the Duren under — both picks are based on the market slightly overvaluing big-man rebounds in tonight's slate. Note: this is a marginal edge and unit size should reflect that.

Banchero's model median of 21.1 points lands just below the 21.5 line, with the normal distribution (σ ≈ 7) giving the Under roughly 52-53% probability. At -115 the edge is essentially breakeven — included here to round out the slate but sizing should be minimal (0.5u or less). Detroit's defense has been improved over the second half of the season, and Banchero's usage rate ticks down slightly in road games. Available at Sportsbook Rhode Island.

Sengun projects for 5.9 assists at the median, nudging the Over 5.5 to about 56% probability. At -126 (implies 55.8%) the edge is razor-thin — essentially a coin flip with a marginal model lean. Included as the sixth pick to complete the slate; treat this as a very light play. Sengun has been distributing the ball well out of the pick-and-roll this season, averaging 6.2 assists over his last 10 games. Available at Sportsbook Rhode Island.
